prs.title: [MNT] Replace validate_data with check_array in predict methods
prs.body:
#### Reference Issues/PRs
N/A
#### What does this implement/fix? Explain your changes.

This PR replaces validate_data with check_array in the predict, predict_proba, and regressor predict methods.

This makes the input validation in prediction steps more consistent with how sklearn typically handles it.

#### What should a reviewer concentrate their feedback on?

- Whether using check_array instead of validate_data in prediction methods is appropriate
- Any potential impact on existing prediction behavior

#### Did you add any tests for the change?

No additional tests were added. The changes were verified by running the classifier and ensuring predictions work as expected.

#### Any other comments?
This is a small cleanup to make the prediction methods follow sklearn conventions more closely.

#### PR checklist


- [x] The PR title starts with [MNT]
- [ ] Added/modified tests
- [ ] Used pre-commit hooks

prs.title: [BUG] Fix AptaNetPipeline.fit to return self (sklearn compatibility)
prs.body:
#### Reference Issues/PRs
Fixes #448

#### What does this implement/fix? Explain your changes.

The fit() method of AptaNetPipeline was returning None, which is inconsistent with sklearn estimators.
This change makes fit() return self, so method chaining works as expected.
Doctests were also updated to reflect this behavior.

#### What should a reviewer concentrate their feedback on?

Correctness of returning self from fit()
Whether the doctest updates are appropriate
Consistency with sklearn-style estimator behavior

#### Did you add any tests for the change?

Yes, added a unit test to verify that fit() returns the pipeline instance.

#### Any other comments?

All tests pass locally except one online loader test that fails due to SSL/network issues, which seems unrelated to this change.

#### PR checklist
- [x] The PR title starts with either [ENH], [MNT], [DOC], or [BUG].
- [x] Added/modified tests
- [ ] Used pre-commit hooks when committing to ensure that code is compliant with hooks.

prs.title: Fix UnboundLocalError in ClassroomNotificationsViewset.list when filter_queryset raises DatabaseError

labels.name: APP: Coach
labels.description: Re: Coach App (lessons, quizzes, groups, reports, etc.)

prs.body:
## Summary
This PR fixes an issue in `ClassroomNotificationsViewset.list` where `queryset` can be referenced before it was assigned.
If `filter_queryset()` raises a `DatabaseError` or `OperationalError`,`repair_sqlite_db()` is triggered, but `queryset` is not reassigned before it is used later in the method, which results in an error :
                                    `UnboundLocalError: local variable 'queryset' referenced before assignment`
This change ensures that `queryset` is always defined by assigning an empty queryset after the database repair:
                     queryset = self.get_queryset().none()

With this fix, the `/coach/api/notifications/` endpoint no longer crashes
with a 500 error and instead returns a valid response.



## References
Fixes #14322



## Reviewer guidance
1. Start Kolibri locally from source.
2. Simulate a `DatabaseError` inside `filter_queryset()`.
3. Navigate to the Coach page.
4. Verify that the `/coach/api/notifications/` endpoint no longer raises
   `UnboundLocalError`.
5. Confirm that the endpoint returns a valid response instead of a 500 error.

prs.title: added footer
prs.body:
Added a semantic <footer> section to the website with three columns: About, Contact, and Social Links.

Styled the footer to match existing site design (green headings, centered text, dark background).

Fixed the overlapping issue of the footer with the cards by:

Replacing fixed heights (height: 100vh for .container and height: 600px for .card) with flexible/minimum heights.

Using flex: 1 on .container to allow it to grow and push the footer naturally.

Updated CSS to ensure responsive layout for both cards and footer.

Footer now remains at the bottom of the page without overlapping content.



Changes Made:

HTML: Added <footer> section with .footer-container and .footer-bottom.

CSS: Updated .container and .card to use flex and min-height instead of fixed heights. Added footer styling.



Impact:

Improves UI by adding a proper footer.

Resolves layout issues where footer was overlapping the Dinner or other cards.

Ensures responsive behavior on different screen sizes.




issues :
fixes issues #2

prs.title: Images rendered Properly.
prs.body:
What this PR does

Moved the imgs/ folder into the public/ directory.

No code changes were made — only file relocation.

🧠 Reason for this change

In Vite-based projects, static assets (like images) must be inside the public/ folder to be correctly served in both development and production.

This ensures images can be accessed via /imgs/... without 404 errors.

issue fixes:
close #90

prs.title: added contact us form
prs.body:
### What does this PR do?
- Adds a new "Contact Us" page with a functional form layout.
- Includes fields for Name, Email, and Message.
- Provides placeholders and required validation for Name and Email.
- Informative header and description to guide users.

### How to test
1. Open `contact.html` in a browser.
2. Verify that the form fields (Name, Email, Message) appear correctly.
3. Check that Name and Email are required before submitting.
4. Ensure the page is responsive on different screen sizes.

### Notes
- The form currently does not have a backend action (no actual email submission).
- Styling is linked via `contact.css` and can be updated as needed.

prs.title: Clamp list-follow card notes to 3 lines to prevent overflow (#11174)
prs.body:
<!-- What issue does this PR close? -->
Closes #11174
<!-- What does this PR achieve?-->
fix the overflow problem and solves issue 1174


### Technical
This PR adds a CSS rule in the `static/css/components/list-follow.less` file to prevent long text in list notes from overflowing outside the card container.  

It modifies the `.list-notes` class as follows:
```css
.list-notes {
    overflow-wrap: break-word; /* Allows long words to break onto the next line */
    word-break: break-word;    /* Ensures unbreakable strings (like URLs) wrap */
    white-space: pre-wrap;     /* Preserves line breaks and spaces */
}

issues.title: Fix AptaNetPipeline.fit() not returning self (sklearn compatibility)
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**

The fit() method of AptaNetPipeline returns None instead of the estimator instance.
This breaks compatibility with the expected scikit-learn API, where fit() should return self to allow method chaining.

**To Reproduce**

import numpy as np
from pyaptamer.aptanet import AptaNetPipeline
aptamer = "AGCTTAGCGTACAGCTTAAAAGGGTTTCCCCTGCCCGCGTAC"
protein = "ACDEFGHIKLMNPQRSTVWYACDEFGHIKLMNPQRSTVWY"
X = [(aptamer, protein) for _ in range(10)]
y = np.array([0, 1] * 5, dtype=np.float32)
pipe = AptaNetPipeline()
result = pipe.fit(X, y)
print(type(result)) # returns <class 'NoneType'>


**Expected behavior**

fit() should return the estimator instance (self), consistent with scikit-learn conventions.
This enables method chaining, such as:

pipe.fit(X, y).predict(X)

**Additional context**
The issue appears to be in `pyaptamer/aptanet/_pipeline.py`, inside the `fit()` method.
This appears to be a small API inconsistency and can be fixed by adding return self at the end of the fit() method.
Happy to open a PR with tests if this aligns with the intended design.

**Versions**


import pyaptamer
print(pyaptamer.__version__)

Output
0.1.0a1

issues.title: Potential UnboundLocalError in ClassroomNotificationViewset.list() when filter_queryset() raises DB exception
issues.body: ❌ **This issue is not open for contribution. Visit Contributing guidelines** to learn about the contributing process and how to find suitable issues. **Target branch:** develop ## Observed behavior In ClassroomNotificationViewset.list() which is inside kolibri/plugins/coach/api.py . If filter_queryset() raises a DatabaseError or OperationalError the exception is caught and repair_sqlite_db() is executed . While reviewing the error-handling path, I simulated a `DatabaseError` inside `filter_queryset()` to test the recovery behavior. In this scenario, `queryset` is not reassigned before being used later in the method. This results in: UnboundLocalError: local variable 'queryset' referenced before assignment Because of this, the request to `/coach/api/notifications/` returns a 500 Internal Server Error. ## Errors and logs ``` ERROR Internal Server Error: /coach/api/notifications/ Traceback (most recent call last): File ".../rest_framework/views.py", line 506, in dispatch response = handler(request, *args, **kwargs) File ".../kolibri/plugins/coach/api.py", line 227, in list "results": self.serialize(queryset), UnboundLocalError: local variable 'queryset' referenced before assignment INFO 127.0.0.1 - "GET /coach/api/notifications/" 500 ``` … ## Expected behavior When a DatabaseError or OperationalError happens inside filter_queryset(), the endpoint currently returns a 500 error. After repair_sqlite_db() is triggered, queryset is not reassigned, and later in the list() method it is still used. Because of that, it crashes with: UnboundLocalError: local variable 'queryset' referenced before assignment Instead of crashing, the endpoint should recover properly and return a valid JSON response. ## User-facing consequences When this happens, the Coach page triggers repeated 500 errors for the notifications endpoint. Because of this, classroom notifications do not load properly. The browser console keeps showing failed requests, and the page continues sending repeated requests to the notifications endpoint. For coaches, this means recent learner progress notifications are not visible … ## Steps to reproduce 1. Start Kolibri locally from source. 2. Temporarily modify `filter_queryset()` to raise a `DatabaseError` (to test the error-handling path). 3. Restart the server. 4. Log in as a user with Coach permissions. 5. Navigate to the Coach page. 6. Observe the request to `/coach/api/notifications/` returning 500 Internal Server Error. … ## Context Application version: Kolibri develop (running from source) OS: macOS Python: 3.10 Browser: Chrome 145.0.7632.117 (arm64)
